html:
ExamineWhen hit by Boomerang

“You have to catch it once you throw it.”

-

The Boomerang is a craftable Ranged Weapon. It requires 1 Boards, 1 Silk, and 1 Charcoal to craft and an Alchemy Engine to prototype.

The Boomerang returns to the player after being thrown. The player must catch it by holding down the action key (default Space key) while it is on its way back. If it isn't caught after being thrown, it will hit the player, causing damage and losing twice the durability on return, and the player will have to pick it up from the ground in order to use it again.

The Boomerang will return to the player even if the player moves from the original throwing spot. It will not hit a target if the target moves out of range before the Boomerang reaches it, but it will always return to the player no matter how far they are.

Skins

Main article: Belongings

  • The Woven - Elegant Bananarang skin was introduced in the An Eye for An Eye update in 2021, as a crossover collaboration with Re-Logic's Terraria. Its rarity was Inspired - Elegant initially, but received change in August 19, 2022.
  • The Loyal Maiming Crescent skin can be obtained by redeeming the DST: Welcome Gift on the Playstation Store during the Don't Starve Together Console Edition PS5 Launch event.
Woven - Elegant
An Eye for an Eye Collection
Bananarang
An appealing weapon to those who'd rather not part with their snacks.
Woven - Elegant
Year of the Pig King Collection
Tiger Boomerang
Grab this tiger boomerang by the tail.
See ingame
Loyal
Maiming Crescent
Deadliness and fun, all bound into one.

Tips

  • The player can have more than one Boomerang out at a time. The action key must be held for each returning Boomerang, however.
  • The Boomerang kills Rabbits and Birds in one throw (except for characters who don't hit as hard, such as Wendy and Wes), making it a great tool for gathering Morsels and Feathers. However, nearby Rabbits and Birds will run away after a death, and additionally, killing them raises the player's naughtiness.
    • Birds are perhaps more preferable to kill with a Boomerang since Rabbits can still be caught easily with other means such as Traps, and the objects needed to craft items to kill birds are more demanding than Rabbit Traps.
  • When playing as Wolfgang, he may do more or less damage depending on his hunger level, or mightiness in DST, He also receives the same damage multiplier from uncaught Boomerangs.
  • The Boomerang serves much the same purpose on the console versions of the game, though because of the auto targeting system, it is not a recommended weapon. One must not stand near anything interactive (Grass, Flowers, Flint, etc.) and wait for the Boomerang to be selected on its way back before quickly pressing "X" to catch it. This can be hard to do, and if the player's health is too low, it can easily cause death.
  • Because of the low damage, the easy to craft recipe, and durability the boomerang is most useful at aggroing fast mobs such as Koalefant and Volt Goats or to kill Birds and Rabbits.

Trivia

  • If the player dies from being struck by a Boomerang, the Morgue will show the Character's name under "Cause of Death".
  • The Boomerang's description ("It comes from a land down under.") is a reference to Australia's nickname, the "Land Down Under", and also to a famous song by the Australian band Men at Work.
  • Being struck by a Boomerang while on a Boat in the Shipwrecked DLC, will cause damage to the Boat rather than to the players.
  • When playing PvP in Don't Starve Together, players are able to catch Boomerangs thrown at them.
  • In Don't Starve Together, failing to catch a boomerang will mark the receiver as an attacker, causing other characters to see them as such if examined.
  • As shown by his examination quote, Warly seems to be affected by separation anxiety disorder. An individual affected by it experiences excessive anxiety regarding separation from home and/or from people to whom the individual has a strong emotional attachment (e.g., a parent, caregiver, significant other, or siblings).
    • This is reinforced when Warly is shown to feel a strong attachment to his maman in the animated short Taste of Home.

Bugs

  • If a Bird targeted by a Boomerang flies away before it hits it, the Boomerang will go to the spot where the Bird was and spin perpetually. If picked up and placed back down without being thrown again, it will continue to move to the same spot. This is a very rare occurrence, since most of the time when the bird does fly away, the Boomerang will just return immediately.
  • If an Eyeplant eats a Boomerang mid-throw, the player will be harmed upon killing the Lureplant if the Boomerang is undigested.
  • Boomerangs may fly away instead of returning to the player after hitting its target. It will return to the player later (even after several days) and will still damage the player if not caught.
  • If the player is currently equipping The Lazy Forager, the boomerang will be added back to the inventory as soon as it is thrown.

Assets

Maiming Crescent

  • Equipped
  • Dropped

Gallery

  • Boomerang Equipped
  • Original HD icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Wolfgang holding a Boomerang.
  • A thrown Boomerang.
  • A bugged Boomerang.
  • Boomerang returning from offscreen non-stop.
  • Boomerang as seen in the poster for the Progress! update.
Tools and WeaponsCraftable Items

DSTDSRoGSWHam

ExamineWhen hit by Boomerang
“Aerodynamical!”
–Wilson
“It's not the most exciting weapon.”
–Willow
“Boom! A rang! Ha!”
–Wolfgang
“Death returns to the sender.”
–Wendy
“REUSABLE PROJECTILE.”
–WX-78
“It is a flat aerofoil.”
–Wickerbottom
“That looks hard to throw.”
–Woodie
“Beats getting your hands dirty.”
–Maxwell
“The unbalanced aerodynamic forces acting upon it create an eliptical airborne path.”
–Wagstaff
“För flinging at föes!”
–Wigfrid
“Boomerangarangarang!”
–Webber
“They say if you love something, you should let it go.”
–Walani
“Oh good. I have separation anxiety.”
–Warly
“I prefer somethin' thet don't come back at me head.”
–Woodlegs
“RETURN YOU THITHER?”
–Wilba
“Returny stick”
–Wormwood
“If you love something set it free. If it comes back it's probably a boomerang.”
–Wheeler
“It's great at comebacks. Ha!”
–Winona
“What goes around comes around, they say, they say!”
–Wortox
“Come-back stick!”
–Wurt
“Fetch, Woby!”
–Walter
“I think I remember how to use this... yes, it's coming back to me!”
–Wanda
“Ow! I should try to catch that!”
–Wilson, when hit by their own Boomerang
“Stupid boomerang!”
–Willow, when hit by their own Boomerang
“Why you hurt me throwing stick?”
–Wolfgang, when hit by their own Boomerang
“OW! That was karma.”
–Wendy, when hit by their own Boomerang
“ERROR, CATCH FAILED”
–WX-78, when hit by their own Boomerang
“I misjudged the timing of its return.”
–Wickerbottom, when hit by their own Boomerang
“Sorry! Clumsy me!”
–Woodie, when hit by their own Boomerang
“Ow! Clumsy!”
–Maxwell, when hit by their own Boomerang
“Missed! My calculations must be off.”
–Wagstaff, when hit by their own Boomerang
“Urg! I must master the curved weapön.”
–Wigfrid, when hit by their own Boomerang
“It hurts us when we don't catch it.”
–Webber, when hit by their own Boomerang
“Ow! Stop hitting yourself, Walani.”
–Walani, when hit by their own Boomerang
“Ouch! Stupid, horrible thing!”
–Warly, when hit by their own Boomerang
“Infernal weapon!”
–Woodlegs, when hit by their own Boomerang
“RETURN-ETH STICK”
–Wilba, when hit by their own Boomerang
“Whoops. Missed it.”
–Wormwood, when hit by their own Boomerang
“Ouch! That came back to haunt me.”
–Wheeler, when hit by their own Boomerang
“I'll catch you next time! Ow...”
–Winona, when hit by their own Boomerang
“Hyuyu!”
–Wortox, when hit by their own Boomerang
“Glorp! Come-back stick hurts.”
–Wurt, when hit by their own Boomerang
“Hey, who threw that?! Oh, wait...”
–Walter, when hit by their own Boomerang
“What goes around comes around.”
–Wanda, when hit by their own Boomerang
“You have to catch it once you throw it.”
-
The Boomerang is a craftable Ranged Weapon. It requires 1 Boards, 1 Silk, and 1 Charcoal to craft and an Alchemy Engine to prototype.
The Boomerang returns to the player after being thrown. The player must catch it by holding down the action key (default Space key) while it is on its way back. If it isn't caught after being thrown, it will hit the player, causing damage and losing twice the durability on return, and the player will have to pick it up from the ground in order to use it again.
The Boomerang will return to the player even if the player moves from the original throwing spot. It will not hit a target if the target moves out of range before the Boomerang reaches it, but it will always return to the player no matter how far they are.
Skins[edit | edit source]

The Woven - Elegant Bananarang skin was introduced in the An Eye for An Eye update in 2021, as a crossover collaboration with Re-Logic's Terraria. Its rarity was Inspired - Elegant initially, but received change in August 19, 2022.
The Loyal Maiming Crescent skin can be obtained by redeeming the DST: Welcome Gift on the Playstation Store during the Don't Starve Together Console Edition PS5 Launch event.

Tips[edit | edit source]
The player can have more than one Boomerang out at a time. The action key must be held for each returning Boomerang, however.
The Boomerang kills Rabbits and Birds in one throw (except for characters who don't hit as hard, such as Wendy and Wes), making it a great tool for gathering Morsels and Feathers. However, nearby Rabbits and Birds will run away after a death, and additionally, killing them raises the player's naughtiness.
Birds are perhaps more preferable to kill with a Boomerang since Rabbits can still be caught easily with other means such as Traps, and the objects needed to craft items to kill birds are more demanding than Rabbit Traps.
When playing as Wolfgang, he may do more or less damage depending on his hunger level, or mightiness in DST, He also receives the same damage multiplier from uncaught Boomerangs.
The Boomerang serves much the same purpose on the console versions of the game, though because of the auto targeting system, it is not a recommended weapon. One must not stand near anything interactive (Grass, Flowers, Flint, etc.) and wait for the Boomerang to be selected on its way back before quickly pressing "X" to catch it. This can be hard to do, and if the player's health is too low, it can easily cause death.
Because of the low damage, the easy to craft recipe, and durability the boomerang is most useful at aggroing fast mobs such as Koalefant and Volt Goats or to kill Birds and Rabbits.

Being struck by a Boomerang while on a Boat in the Shipwrecked DLC, will cause damage to the Boat rather than to the players.
When playing PvP in Don't Starve Together, players are able to catch Boomerangs thrown at them.
In Don't Starve Together, failing to catch a boomerang will mark the receiver as an attacker, causing other characters to see them as such if examined.
As shown by his examination quote, Warly seems to be affected by separation anxiety disorder. An individual affected by it experiences excessive anxiety regarding separation from home and/or from people to whom the individual has a strong emotional attachment (e.g., a parent, caregiver, significant other, or siblings).
This is reinforced when Warly is shown to feel a strong attachment to his maman in the animated short Taste of Home.
Bugs[edit | edit source]
If a Bird targeted by a Boomerang flies away before it hits it, the Boomerang will go to the spot where the Bird was and spin perpetually. If picked up and placed back down without being thrown again, it will continue to move to the same spot. This is a very rare occurrence, since most of the time when the bird does fly away, the Boomerang will just return immediately.

Boomerangs may fly away instead of returning to the player after hitting its target. It will return to the player later (even after several days) and will still damage the player if not caught.
If the player is currently equipping The Lazy Forager, the boomerang will be added back to the inventory as soon as it is thrown.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Boomerang là một loại vũ khí tầm xa có thể chế tạo được. Nó yêu cầu 1 Ván ép, 1 Tơ và 1 Than để chế tạo và Máy Luyện kim để tạo nguyên mẫu.

It requires 1 [[Boards]], 1 [[Silk]], and 1 [[Charcoal]] to craft and an [[Alchemy Engine]] to prototype. The Boomerang returns to the player after being thrown. The player must catch it by holding down the action key (default Space key) while it is on its way back. The Boomerang will return to the player even if the player moves from the original throwing spot. It will not hit a target if the target moves out of range before the Boomerang reaches it, but it will always return to the player no matter how far they are. __NOTOC__ == [[File:Skins Icon.png|32px|link=]] Skins == {{Main|Belongings#Weapons Skins|Belongings}} * The '''Woven - Elegant Bananarang''' skin was introduced in the ''[[An Eye for An Eye]]'' update in 2021, as a crossover collaboration with Re-Logic's ''Terraria''. Its rarity was '''Inspired - Elegant''' initially, but received change in August 19, 2022.
* The '''Loyal Maiming Crescent''' skin can be obtained by redeeming the [https://store.playstation.com/en-us/product/UP2107-CUSA04236_00-WELCOM0000000000 DST: Welcome Gift] on the Playstation Store during the Don't Starve Together Console Edition PS5 Launch event. {{SkinInfo |image = Bananarang Icon.png |rarity = Woven - Elegant |collection = An Eye for an Eye Collection |name = Bananarang |description = An appealing weapon to those who'd rather not part with their snacks. }} {{SkinInfo |image = Tiger Boomerang Icon.png |rarity = Woven - Elegant |collection = Year of the Pig King Collection |name = Tiger Boomerang |description = Grab this tiger boomerang by the tail. |ingame = Tiger Boomerang Wilson.png }} {{SkinInfo |image = Maiming Crescent Icon.png |rarity = Loyal |name = Maiming Crescent |description = Deadliness and fun, all bound into one. }}
== {{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips == * The player can have more than one Boomerang out at a time. The action key must be held for each returning Boomerang, however. * The Boomerang kills [[Rabbit]]s and [[Birds]] in one throw (except for characters who don't hit as hard, such as [[Wendy]] and [[Wes]]), making it a great tool for gathering [[Morsel]]s and [[Feather]]s. However, nearby Rabbits and Birds will run away after a death, and additionally, killing them raises the player's [[naughtiness]]. ** Birds are perhaps more preferable to kill with a Boomerang since Rabbits can still be caught easily with other means such as Traps, and the objects needed to craft items to kill birds are more demanding than Rabbit Traps. * When playing as [[Wolfgang]], he may do more or less damage depending on his hunger level, or mightiness in DST, He also receives the same damage multiplier from uncaught Boomerangs. * The Boomerang serves much the same purpose on the console versions of the game, though because of the auto targeting system, it is not a recommended weapon. One must not stand near anything interactive ([[Grass]], [[Flowers]], [[Flint]], etc.) and wait for the Boomerang to be selected on its way back before quickly pressing "X" to catch it. This can be hard to do, and if the player's health is too low, it can easily cause death. * Because of the low damage, the easy to craft recipe, and durability the [[boomerang]] is most useful at aggroing fast mobs such as [[Koalefant]] and [[Volt Goat]]s or to kill [[Birds]] and [[Rabbit]]s. == {{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * If the player dies from being struck by a Boomerang, the [[Morgue]] will show the Character's name under "Cause of Death". * The Boomerang's description ("It comes from a land down under.") is a reference to Australia's nickname, the "Land Down Under", and also to a famous song by the Australian band [[wikipedia:Men at Work|Men at Work]]. * Being struck by a Boomerang while on a [[Boats|Boat]] in the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC, will cause damage to the Boat rather than to the players. * When playing PvP in ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', players are able to catch Boomerangs thrown at them. * In ''Don't Starve Together'', failing to catch a boomerang will mark the receiver as an attacker, causing other characters to see them as such if examined. * As shown by his examination quote, [[Warly]] seems to be affected by [[wikipedia:Separation_anxiety_disorder|separation anxiety disorder]]. An individual affected by it experiences excessive anxiety regarding separation from home and/or from people to whom the individual has a strong emotional attachment (e.g., a parent, caregiver, significant other, or siblings). ** This is reinforced when Warly is shown to feel a strong attachment to his maman in the animated short ''[[Animated shorts and trailers#Taste of Home|Taste of Home]]''. == {{Pic32|Mosquito}} Bugs == * If a [[Birds|Bird]] targeted by a Boomerang flies away before it hits it, the Boomerang will go to the spot where the Bird was and spin perpetually. If picked up and placed back down without being thrown again, it will continue to move to the same spot. This is a very rare occurrence, since most of the time when the bird does fly away, the Boomerang will just return immediately. * If an [[Eyeplant]] eats a Boomerang mid-throw, the player will be harmed upon killing the [[Lureplant]] if the Boomerang is undigested. * Boomerangs may fly away instead of returning to the player after hitting its target. It will return to the player later (even after several days) and will still damage the player if not caught. * If the player is currently equipping [[The Lazy Forager]], the boomerang will be added back to the inventory as soon as it is thrown. == [[File:Research Notes.png|32px]] Assets == |-| Maiming Crescent= Maiming Crescent Equipped.png|Equipped Maiming Crescent Dropped.png|Dropped == {{Pic32|Blueprint}} Gallery == Boomerang Equipped.png|Boomerang Equipped CD boomerang.png|Original HD icon from Bonus Materials from [[CD Don't Starve]].
